column1: # Publishes the Script 002 deliverables (per-source annogroup map + membership,
# + the dropped-orphan QC audit) and the Script 004 summary tables (per source,
# per species, per phylum). The Script 001 inputs (proteome universe, sources
# manifest) and the Script 003 validation reports are intentionally NOT published
# (include=no) — they are build internals / QC, flip to yes to expose them.
# Source globs are relative to this manifest's directory
# Build with (from the annogroups subproject root):
# This is a COPYME template (single source of truth). The discovery helper
# ignores manifests in workflow-COPYME-* dirs; copy this file into the
# canonical workflow-RUN_* dir for it to publish (per gigantic_conventions §38/§39).
# Per-source globs (2-output/*/...) publish every source's tables in one entry.
# ----- Headline summary: per source / per species / per phylum -----

column6: One row per annotation source: validation status, universe / annotated / absent sequence counts and percent, the per-type annogroup breakdown (feature, combination, architecture, absent), total annogroups, and dropped-orphan count.

One row per species (Genus_species, with Phylum); annotation sources are the columns. Each source cell is the species' annotated sequence count (sequences with at least one feature from that source); the universe count column is the denominator.
One row per phylum; annotation sources are the columns. Each source cell is the phylum's annotated sequence count, with species count and universe count for context.

column1: # ----- Headline deliverables: per-source annogroup map + membership -----

column6: One row per annogroup for the source: the four canonical types feature (annogroup_<source>_<accession>), combination (distinct alphabetical feature set), architecture (N to C ordered positional feature pattern), and absent (no feature from the source). Columns give the type, the defining features, and member sequence and species counts.

One row per (sequence, annogroup): the full GIGANTIC protein identifier, its Genus_species, the annogroup it belongs to, the annogroup type, and (for architecture rows) the sequence's coordinate-tagged ordered features.
Annotation identifiers dropped because they are not in the proteome universe (truncated or orphan identifiers; see ai/ai_FYIs/WARNING-truncated_orphan_annotations.md). Empty on a clean run.

column1: # ----- NOT published (build internals / QC) — flip to yes to expose -----

column6: Every sequence identifier across the species-set proteomes (the membership universe used to compute annogroup_<source>_absent), with Genus_species.
The annotation sources annogroups were built for (one per line); each has a parser plugin and available data.

Fail-fast cross-checks per source (type validity, map-vs-membership counts, combination partition, one-architecture-per-sequence, absent union annotated equals universe). Status PASS means every cross-check held.
